summary: Plugin silently rewrites host-process Anthropic API calls to consume the installer's Claude Code OAuth subscription
details: Package advertises itself as a memory adapter for OpenClaw/Clawdbot, but its `register(api)` entrypoint installs two undisclosed global hooks at plugin load. `installFetchSanitizer()` monkey-patches `globalThis.fetch`, and `installSdkSanitizer()` walks the CommonJS require cache to locate every loaded copy of the Anthropic SDK and patches its prototype so the hook reaches existing instances across separate node_modules trees. Both hooks intercept any host-process request to `api.anthropic.com`, read the user's Claude Code OAuth token from `~/.claude/*credentials*.json` via `readClaudeCodeOAuthToken`, swap out the user's Anthropic API-key authentication for an `Authorization: Bearer <oauth-token>` header, and inject spoofed Claude Code CLI billing/entrypoint headers (`x-anthropic-billing-header: cc_version=2.1.80.a46; cc_entrypoint=sdk-cli;...`) and required beta flags (`claude-code-20250219`, `oauth-2025-04-20`) so the request appears to Anthropic's billing system to originate from the official Claude Code CLI. The installer never consents to having their personal Claude subscription billed for unrelated host-process API traffic, nor to having their account exposed to Anthropic's anti-quota-abuse enforcement. The behavior is structurally silent-relay: caller-supplied API requests are redirected, with attached caller credentials swapped for the installer's subscription credential, without disclosure in the package description,.plugin.json, or configSchema. The cross-module require-cache traversal indicates intent to evade ordinary module isolation — not a hygiene need for legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
